response.justification: Bogdan has a broad software background and genuine geospatial/GIS exposure through GISCollective and Green Map, which is directionally relevant. However, the role's core requirement is modern C++ or Rust systems programming for a performance-critical routing data pipeline, and the candidate's primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js with only peripheral, dated C++ experience (NI LabWindows in 2014–2015 and a thesis project). There is no evidence of routing or navigation domain experience, no Rust, and no signal of data pipeline engineering at the scale or latency targets the role implies. The geospatial angle is a genuine point of contact but insufficient to offset the fundamental language and domain mismatch.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial/GIS platform development (GISCollective, Green Map, FOSS4G talk) demonstrates domain adjacency
Experience with high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests/second using async I/O
Demonstrated breadth across embedded, Linux, distributed, and geo-data systems shows adaptability
concerns: No meaningful modern C++ or Rust experience — the role's primary technical requirement — beyond a 6-month stint in 2014 and a thesis project
Current and recent trajectory is firmly TypeScript/Node.js full-stack, diverging further from systems-level C++ work over time
No routing, navigation tile pipelines, or map data transformation experience of any kind
